description_long: This object consists of three stylized mushrooms with vibrant red-orange caps and pale stems, arranged side by side. The caps display a gradient from deep red at the center to yellowish at the edges, with the left and right mushrooms featuring raised, rounded warty spots, while the middle one is smoother with subtle speckling. The stems are cylindrical and beige, tapering slightly toward the base, and appear solid and sturdy. The rightmost mushroom has a more conical cap with prominent white spots, reminiscent of the Amanita muscaria species. The overall surface of the caps is matte with a slight texture, and the stems have a smooth, almost wooden appearance. These mushrooms are depicted as if they could be found in a park or garden setting, adding a whimsical, natural element to an urban environment.
description: A group of three stylized red-orange mushrooms with warty and spotted caps and pale, smooth stems, arranged side by side. The caps feature gradient coloring and distinct surface textures, evoking a whimsical, natural look.
description_view_0: Three mushrooms are shown side by side, highlighting the differences in cap shapes and surface textures, with the left and right mushrooms displaying warty spots and the middle one appearing smoother.
description_view_1: The mushrooms are aligned front-to-back, emphasizing the cap profiles and the vertical alignment of the stems, with the rightmost mushroom's conical cap and white spots most visible.
description_view_2: The mushrooms are viewed from the opposite side, showing the smoother cap of the middle mushroom and the warty, rounded caps of the left and right mushrooms.
description_view_3: The mushrooms are stacked front-to-back, with the smallest, most conical mushroom in front, highlighting the cap textures and the gradual size increase from front to back.
attribute_cap_pattern: warty and spotted with subtle speckling
